lines: If your mind is already racing... [short pause] [short pause] that's okay. [short pause] We're going to slow it down. [short pause] Not by fighting it. [short pause] By breathing underneath it.

Inhale through your nose for four... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] And exhale through your mouth, long and slow... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven... [short pause] [short pause] eight.

Again. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] And out, slowly... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven... [short pause] [short pause] eight.

In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven... [short pause] [short pause] eight.

One more. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four. [short pause] And let everything go... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven... [short pause] [short pause] eight.

That long exhale activates your vagus nerve... [short pause] [short pause] the longest nerve in your body, running from your brainstem to your gut. [short pause] It tells your nervous system: you're safe. [short pause] Not a thought. [short pause] A signal. [short pause] Your body can calm your mind when your mind won't calm itself.
Now. [short pause] Jesus, the night before he died. [short pause] Speaking to his closest friends, who were about to watch their world collapse:
"Peace I leave with you; my peace I give you. [short pause] I do not give to you as the world gives. [short pause] Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid." John 14, verse 27.

My peace I give you. [short pause] Not the world's peace... [short pause] [short pause] which depends on everything going right. [short pause] His peace... [short pause] [short pause] which exists independent of what happens today. [short pause] That distinction matters. [short pause] The world's peace is conditional. [short pause] Christ's peace is given.

Today might be hard. [short pause] You might already know it... [short pause] [short pause] a meeting, a deadline, a conversation, a result you're waiting for. [short pause] Or maybe it's vaguer than that. [short pause] Just a weight. [short pause] A hum of dread without a clear source.
Name the thing you're anxious about. [short pause] Be as specific as you can.

Now notice something. [short pause] The dread is here. [short pause] But the thing hasn't happened yet. [short pause] Your shoulders are tense, your breathing is shallow, your stomach is tight... [short pause] [short pause] and the event is still in the future. [short pause] You are suffering in advance. [short pause] Your body is responding to a story your mind is telling... [short pause] [short pause] not to what's actually happening in this room, right now.

This isn't your fault. [short pause] Your brain evolved to predict threats. [short pause] It's doing its job. [short pause] But it can't tell the difference between a real danger and a projected one. [short pause] So your body prepares for war... [short pause] [short pause] against a meeting.

What if the story is wrong? [short pause] What if it goes differently than you expect? [short pause] What if you're more capable than the anxiety says? [short pause] These are not wishful thinking. [short pause] They are equally valid possibilities. [short pause] Anxiety just speaks louder.
Let's do something concrete. [short pause] We're going to name three things that are actually true right now.

First... [short pause] [short pause] what can you feel? [short pause] The surface beneath you. [short pause] The temperature of the air. [short pause] The fabric on your skin. [short pause] Name one physical sensation.

Second... [short pause] [short pause] what can you hear? [short pause] Not the thoughts. [short pause] The actual sounds around you. [short pause] Traffic. [short pause] A fan. [short pause] Birds. [short pause] Silence itself. [short pause] Name what you hear.

Third... [short pause] [short pause] what's one thing you know to be true about today? [short pause] Not the catastrophe. [short pause] One simple, verifiable fact. [short pause] You have somewhere to be. [short pause] Someone cares about you. [short pause] You've done hard things before.
Good. [short pause] You just anchored yourself in reality. [short pause] Anxiety lives in projection. [short pause] Peace lives in the present.
Now let's give the weight somewhere to go.

Lord... [short pause] [short pause] you can see what I'm carrying. [short pause] The thing I named and the things I didn't. [short pause] You know the tightness in my chest and the stories my mind keeps spinning. [short pause] I give you the outcome. [short pause] Not because I don't care... [short pause] [short pause] but because holding it is crushing me and it was never mine to control. [short pause] Your peace isn't something I have to manufacture. [short pause] Help me receive it. [short pause] Like sunlight. [short pause] Like breath. [short pause] Like this morning I didn't earn. [short pause] Amen.

Before you go... [short pause] [short pause] build your anchor. [short pause] Something to return to when the anxiety rises today. [short pause] And it might. [short pause] That's okay. [short pause] You'll have this.

Take one slow breath. [short pause] As you exhale... [short pause] [short pause] drop your shoulders. [short pause] Let them fall away from your ears. [short pause] Soften your jaw. [short pause] Unclench your teeth. [short pause] And open your hands, palms up, wherever they are.

Slow breath. [short pause] Soft shoulders. [short pause] Open hands. [short pause] That's your anchor. [short pause] Three seconds, anywhere, anytime. [short pause] In the car, at your desk, before walking into the room. [short pause] Three seconds to return to what's real.
"Peace I leave with you. [short pause] My peace I give you. [short pause] Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid."
That peace goes with you today. [short pause] Not because the day will be easy. [short pause] But because the One who gives it will be present in every moment of it.
Carry the anchor. [short pause] And go in peace.
